content.question: Does parental preference for selective schools reflect genuine educational value (e.g., peer effects, teaching quality in tracks) or perceived prestige, and would this preference persist if non-selective schools achieved equivalent academic outcomes?
content.reduction_path: Conduct large-scale parent preference experiments and discrete choice surveys in schools offering both tracked and untracked pathways. Track actual school choices and student outcomes longitudinally. Qualitative interviews exploring whether prestige preferences change after non-selective schools improve achievement and social cohesion metrics.
